entry.title: Re: Connect SAP BTP Trial account with Eclipse
entry.content: same here - any ideas?

entry.title: Re: Output window shows no information in Visual Studio Extension - release 2505
entry.content: @cianbarrett I have already opened a case with SAP. Case is 651677/2025

entry.title: Document Item text mandatory for scrapping in manage stock application
entry.content:
Document Item text to be mandatory for scrapping in "Manage stock" application, Highlighted in screenshot below,

we have made the field mandatory through configuration shown in screenshot below,

but this is mandatory field reflecting in MIGO (Post goods movement) Application but not in "Manage stock" application.

We need it to be applicable in "Manage stock" application, If any other configuration is missed in this case please highlight.

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Request for Belgium - ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
entry.content: Any update yet?

entry.title: Re: Output window shows no information in Visual Studio Extension - release 2505
entry.content: I do get the message "Debugger started for user XXX", but that's it. No more messages while debugging.

entry.title: Re: Send PDF from SFTP adapter to HTTP via SAP CPI
entry.content: Hi Vikranth,

entry.title: Authorization für SAP Readiness Check Analysis
entry.content:
Hi

I am the user administrator for Deloitte Switzerland. A user wants access to the Readiness Check Analysis




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Authorization für SAP Readiness Check Analysis
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

S-user IDs must have the authorizations below to view and maintain data in the SAP Readiness Check application in SAP for Me, as outlined in KBA  3455928 - Authorizations required for the Readiness Check application - SAP for Me

  • Display SAP Readiness Check Analysis (RC_ANA_DIS)
    Enables S-users to view all SAP Readiness Check analyses belonging to the customer number associated with their user ID.

  • Manage SAP Readiness Check Analysis (RC_ANA_MGT)
    Enables S-users to create, edit, delete, and share all SAP Readiness Check analyses belonging to their customer number.

To grant the necessary authorizations to S-users, please refer to KBA 1511008 - How to add or change S-user ID authorizations - SAP for Me

Hope the above information is helpful to you.

Kind regards,
Judy

entry.title: Re: Send PDF from SFTP adapter to HTTP via SAP CPI
entry.content: Hi Vikranth, did you get the solution for this scenario, as i'm also getting 415 error

entry.title: Re: Condition contracts - vendor rebates
entry.content:
Hello Jonathan,

The final settlement will be used to close the contract and offset remaining accruals. To capture the incoming vendor invoice, you have to use t-code WB2R_DOC_ENTRY .

Regards,

Thorsten

entry.title: From SAP Business Suite to SAP Cloud ERP: Realizing the Strategy - AM
entry.content:
In this webcast, SAP expert will show you firsthand how the SAP Business Suite strategy is fully realized within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. We'll delve into how embedded AI technologies significantly enhance user productivity, showcasing practical use cases and outlining the exciting roadmap ahead.

You'll gain valuable insights into how the suite's comprehensive quality is designed to support and scale with your specific business growth requirements. This session is your opportunity to understand why SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is the perfect foundation for your digital transformation journey in this dynamic, technology-driven era.

entry.title: Re: No certification even after a week of exam and support ticket
entry.content: Over 2 weeks now. Still no certificate!

entry.title: Do we need additional licences for label printing in S4Hana Public cloud 2502 ?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are in process to deploy SAP WM on Public cloud 2502, where we want to have label printing enable for Handling Unit, Putaway, Picking, Shipping, 3PL labels etc. Do we need to go for additional licenses to enable label printing in WM public cloud 2502.

Please advise us best possible approach we can have for label printing in Public cloud.

Regards

XXXX

entry.title: Extended Sourcing for Public Sector for S/4 Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
I read about an "Extended Sourcing for Public Sector", that is announced for Q4 2025, in the SAP Industry Solution Portfolio website (ext. link) .

I would like to confirm if it will be available for S/4 Cloud Public Edition. There is an entry for S/4 (ext. link) and S/4 CLD Private (ext. link), but it does not mention S/4 CLD Public specifically.

Another question is what "S/4 PPS" stands for. Inside the links, it mentions "SAP Ariba Sourcing and Procurement for Public Sector in SAP S/4HANA", but it confuses me because it mentions both Ariba and S/4. I would like to know more about this solution, but I found nothing.

entry.title: Community, Purpose, and Technology Together!
entry.content:
Yesterday, the organizing team of SAP Inside Track Fortaleza had the privilege of visiting Associação Estação Luz, a non-profit organization based in Eusébio–CE that plays a vital role in our community.

Since 2004, Estação Luz has been transforming lives through social projects in education, culture, and sports, supporting children, teenagers, adults, and elderly people. Their work truly inspires us, showing how collective effort can change society.

As part of our commitment to social responsibility, SIT Fortaleza is proud to announce a partnership with Estação Luz. A portion of the event’s ticket sales will be donated to support their incredible work.

Even more special, the event’s closing moment will feature a musical presentation by the children of Estação Luz, who will bring their talent and joy to our community of SAP professionals.

This is more than an event; it’s about creating impact beyond technology.

Let’s connect, learn, share knowledge — and also contribute to a better future for our local community.

Join us at SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2025 — technology with purpose!

#SAPCommunity #SITFortaleza #SAPInsideTrack #TechForGood #PurposeDriven #EstacaoLuz #Fortaleza #SAP

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Hi @AshishPandey 

Please note there is a difference between bloggers and helpers. Answering questions on the community is not part of my work. I am always happy to answer questions directly pertaining to my blogs, I want people to understand and potentially learn something, but more than that falls outside of what I do or have time to do. Thank you for your understanding.

Kind regards

entry.title: From SAP Business Suite to SAP Cloud ERP: Realizing the Strategy - PM
entry.content:
In this webcast, SAP expert will show you firsthand how the SAP Business Suite strategy is fully realized within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. We'll delve into how embedded AI technologies significantly enhance user productivity, showcasing practical use cases and outlining the exciting roadmap ahead.

You'll gain valuable insights into how the suite's comprehensive quality is designed to support and scale with your specific business growth requirements. This session is your opportunity to understand why SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is the perfect foundation for your digital transformation journey in this dynamic, technology-driven era.

entry.title: Re: Enable SAP BTP Remote Debugging to Diagnose CAPM Application on Node.js Stack
entry.content: I do not understand why it was necessary to try and provide a condensed version of the referenced blog. The original presents everything more than clearly enough.

entry.title: Re: Workflow approval via Integration Center
entry.content:
Hi @mithilapethe,

as mentioned before, you can do it via APIs, here is the link approveWfRequest | SAP Help Portal. Thought there is a limitation that you need to call approval APIs only behalf of the current approver of the workflow.

As a workaround you can add a step into wf with the dynamic group where you can put API user or change the approver as mentioned in the following article 2970678 - New OData Function Import changeWfRequestApprover - 2H 2020 SuccessFactors release - ECT-132722 - SAP for Me

Hope this helps.

Best regards,

Natalia

entry.title: Read data from the APP screen for editable fields without persisting the data.
entry.content:
Hi, I'm developing RAP UNMANAGED to generate a Fiori Elements app using List Report where I used standard PP CDSs for the order list and its components. In the custom CDS that I read from the Standard CDSs, I created a REASON field to insert a code. After that, the idea is to select the edited line with a new value in the REASON field and use an ACTION.

In the ACTION method, I'm not able to retrieve the data that was edited on the screen. The editable field is not persisted in any table. Is it possible to read the UI data at runtime? This REASON field does not need to be saved in any custom table because we only need it to execute the ACTION process.

Field:

The main question is whether RAP, whether UNMANAGED or MANAGED, can read data edited on the screen that does not need to be persisted in the database?

thanks

entry.title: HSN Code wise report SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear all,

I need a query based report which gives output HSN wise summary report. 

HSNCode, Docentry with fields HSNCode , BaseAmt , IGSTAmt , SGSTAmt , CGStAmt, Tax %

Report include two document : AR Invoice and AR Credit note

SAP Business One #HSN #SAP B1

Regards,

Nikunj Mehta

entry.title: Re: RDDIMPDP failed
entry.content: Maintain the client 000 in SCC4 if its not maintained. You may use se16n GD_EDIT, GD_SAPEDIT in debugger mode to update the table T000

entry.title: Re: Previous year's total cannot be displayed in SAC reports
entry.content:
Thank you for your confirmation. I apologize for my poor explanation,

but I would like the year-to-date amount from one year ago to be displayed in the year and month of the current fiscal year.

I would like to see the cumulative amount for FY2024 displayed in the FY2025 section of the measure “YTD Story” that you created.

In this case, I would like the YTDStory for FY2025 to show the amount for Measure “YTD Story” for FY2024.

I would like the amount 884633 to be displayed instead of the amount 9206895 and the amount 20126700 to be displayed in Q1 2025.

entry.title: Re: Planning Modernization
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing, @fkatzdobler !
I think this architecture is relevant for many BPC customers that are already on or about to embark on their journey to SAP Analytics Cloud as usually, there is a transition period of hybrid planning while some processes are already running in SAP Analytics Cloud and others are not transformed yet. In BDC, you can combine the data and with seamless planning, you shall be able to integrate the data product data into the planning models as well. 

BR
Max

entry.title: Re: Add SAP PI/PO Communication Channel Start/Stop Functionality to Your iFlows
entry.content:
Hello Craig, there is an existing request in Customer Influence for this feature: Need a solution to pause / resume interfaces in bulk for SAP Integration - Cloud Integration . We need a generic solution with a display / filter in a monitor to see in which IFlow with channels are stopped. In the monitor an action button is needed to "stop" or "start" selected chanels in IFlow. To have the right overview is very important for maintenace. In some cases we need to stop much IFlows. Please also note that IFlow can have more than two channels due to splits. The workaround is a good idea, but we use also SAP CI standard content and do not want to change this for this feature. The same point is the overview about start timer and temporary "stop" for maintenance. 

Kind regards, Steffen

entry.title: Re: Task list operation: component assignment (OSS note 4...
entry.content:
Hello there

It's been quite a while since I last used EWB-API. But CM_CL_S_COM_CHANGE should allow you to change component assignments. Make sure you run one of the CM_CL_S_COM_PROV* function modules first. I vaguely remember that there was also a ...RELOAD... function module that played a role. You've checked the sample program, right?

I noticed that you tagged this as EAM/PM. Are you sure you are using one of the supported task list types (N,S & Q)?

Regards
Jan

entry.title: Re: Shift identity management from SAP IDM to SAP IAG with CIS and GRC AC.
entry.content:
Thanks for the insight, Saurabh!!

I agree if SAP IDM is used just to perform risk analysis & provisioning into only the SAP system, then a blend of SAP CIS, IAG & GRC can replace it, accommodating complex scenarios.

Problems appear when you have complex scenarios, as in pending value or logical workflow or calculated provisioning into non-sap systems.

entry.title: Procurement: Sourcing SAC Business content installation Ariba Connection to SAC
entry.content:
Hi All,

Good day!!

I am trying to implement the Business content -> Procurement: Sourcing , which is based on the Ariba System.

I tried to check ways to connect Ariba and SAC but then I got to know that there are no straight forward ways even though it is business content.

Can anyone please help on how to consume this standard Ariba API to SAC? 

I got to know that it can be done using CPI but unfortunately I am not able to find any direct documentations which explains this for business content.

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: Re: Adding Custom Fields to Standard Fiori App of S/4HANA
entry.content:
@Siva45 

Apologies for the delayed response.

If you still need it , I will handle it and send it to you .

entry.title: Can I build an Overview Page (OVP) with JSON data ?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you're doing well. I have a question regarding SAP Fiori and SAP Business Application Studio (BAS).

I'm currently working on a custom application, and I would like to build an Overview Page (OVP). However, instead of using a traditional OData service with annotations (as OVP typically requires), I want to use static or dynamic JSON data as my data source.

My goals are:

  • To build an OVP-style UI (with cards/tiles displaying KPIs or summary information).

  • To use local or remote JSON data (e.g., from a JSON file or API).

  • To develop and test this in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), possibly using freestyle SAPUI5.

My questions are:

  1. Is it possible to build an Overview Page (OVP) using only JSON data (without an OData service)?

  2. If so, are there any best practices or examples on how to bind JSON data to the cards on the OVP?

  3. Can I use mock data (e.g., mockserver) in BAS for this purpose?

  4. Is it necessary to use Fiori Elements, or can I achieve this with a freestyle UI5 application?

Any guidance, sample projects, or documentation links would be really appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Does "HANA System Replication" support the replication between systems with different S4HANA version
entry.content:
Here SAP described the situation for Zero downtime during upgrade:

Use SAP HANA System Replication for Near Zero Downtime Upgrades: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4e9b18c116aa42fc84c7dbfd02111aba/ee3fd9a0c2e74733a74e4ad140fde60b.html?version=2.0.05

Does this support the SAP HANA databased upgrade only? or does it support also S4HANA upgrade?

for example, if we upgrade secondary system to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03, and the primary system is on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02, will the replication still working for primary system to secondary system?

entry.title: Fetching Product Attribute Values Using CTX Formula in Configurator
entry.content:
When using the formula validator inside the configurator in a quote, how can we fetch a product attribute's value using the CTX formula?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Fetching Product Attribute Values Using CTX Formula i...
entry.content:
Please use the below CTX formula in the formula validator:
<*CTX( Product.Attribute(<AttributeName>).Value.Translated )*>

entry.title: Re: RA keys
entry.content: Thanks for your reply , but if they are preconfigured and proposed by SAP , where can i read the detailes settings for them ? if i'm not able to configure or change , i at least need to know the difference between every RA key provided

entry.title: How Quote Expiration Job works?
entry.content:
Expiration job is supposed to start at midnight server time, looping through all quotes depending on the workflow settings and expiring it if date is in past. It is visible through logs that it does not start at exactly midnight server time, and also varies day to day, taking several hours to complete. How does the job works, and why it has differentiation on starting time and amount it takes to conclude?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How Quote Expiration Job works?
entry.content:
Expiration job starts at midnight server time and runs on all tenants - currently, there is only one "worker" for all environments (meaning all customers that share instance, since CPQ is Public Cloud). This job changes quote status to expired for all quotes that Quote Expiration Date custom field has value in the past (if the workflow has been set properly). This runs on all quotes and it does not consider the current status, but only the value of the field (and workflow settings). Due to this, the time is starts on each environment can differ considering the amount of quotes present.

There is an enhancement planned on this feature, to increase the quantity of workers, minimizing time needed and preventing errors, but currently no release date.

entry.title: Re: AIF Namespace /EDCII
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you download the BC set from 2854582?

entry.title: Re: Partner Benefits Catalog for SAP Partners
entry.content: Well, the link is more like: Partner Benefits Catalog

entry.title: Flow accounting documents costs to material valuation - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2502.11)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

We aim to post accounting documents that directly affect material valuation, ensuring that costs are appropriately reflected in material stock values.

We are currently working with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, version 2502.11.

Constraints:

  • Internal Orders and CO Production Orders are not available in this version.

  • WBS Elements cannot be settled directly to materials.

  • Production Orders are planned for implementation in a much later phase.

We have also opened a SAP support ticket regarding this issue.

Has anyone encountered a similar situation? 

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Heitor

entry.title: Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog series focused on the provisioning guide for SAP Business Data Cloud, including its applications and components. As many of you are aware that SAP Business Data Cloud (SAP BDC) is SAP's latest innovation. This blog aims to assist the community in getting started with BDC and understanding its key components. It serves as a comprehensive step-by-step guide to help you set up your systems and manage your tenants effectively.

This series will have 5 parts as follows:

Part 1: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud through SAP4Me
Part 2: Provisioning of SAP Datasphere
Part 3: Provisioning of SAP Analytics Cloud
Part 4: Provisioning of SAP Databricks
Part 5: Installation of Intelligent Apps and Data Packages

#Part 1
In this blog, we will discuss in detail how to provision SAP Business Data Cloud through SAP4Me

SAP Business Data Cloud is a comprehensive Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering that integrates and manages all SAP data, while also effortlessly linking with data from external sources. This empowers business leaders with enhanced insights, enabling them to make more significant and informed decisions.

Users with an administrator role can:

  • Provision the systems for the SAP Business Data Cloud suite within SAP for Me and create a 'Business Data Cloud' system formation.
  • Log in to the SAP Business Data Cloud Cockpit.
  • Manage user access to the SAP Business Data Cloud Cockpit.
  • Install Insight apps to SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Activate data packages for installation in SAP Datasphere.
  • Monitor the connectivity of systems added to the 'Business Data Cloud' formation.

Pre-requisites:
You require an S-User ID. Users with an S-User ID can view and provision the systems for the SAP Business Data Cloud suite within SAP for Me. Please note that this S-user ID should be the one that is linked to the customer account that has the BDC entitlement set attached to its order details.

Step-by-step process to access the Business Data Cloud Card:

These steps will guide how to first provision the BDC Cockpit:

  1. Log on to SAP For Me
  2. Navigate to Portfolio & Products
  3. The SAP Business Data Cloud card in SAP for Me displays SAP Business Data Cloud applications, the number of solutions provisioned for each application, and the planned quota you've assigned to them.
  4. From this card, you can access the dashboard where you can easily provision and integrate the suite's applications through a guided process.
  5. The SAP Business Data Cloud card displays the capacity units enabled for the SAP Business Data Cloud suite and their allocation among the solutions included in SAP Business Data Cloud. The number of capacity units assigned for SAP Business Data Cloud Core is based on the licensed quantity.

    Image 1
  6. On the SAP Business Data Cloud card, click "Go to Applications" to access the Product Suites - Provisioning and Integration dashboard for SAP Business Data Cloud, enabling easy provisioning and integration through a guided process.
  7. The dashboard acts as a central hub, providing a user-friendly, comprehensive, and streamlined installation experience for applications within your SAP Business Data Cloud suite.
  8. Initially, upon opening the Applications tab, all tiles are disabled, and a message indicates the need to enable the SAP Business Data Cloud cockpit. It is highlighted as "Enable Now"

    Image 2

  9. First, enable the BDC Core product by providing a Name and allocating it to a Resource Group. Create the Resource Group if it doesn't exist yet
  10. Click the "Next" button
  11. In the "Configure Parameter" section, select the region, data center, and the owner's email
    Note: Ensure SAP Business Data Cloud Core and its solutions are provisioned in the same data center
  12. Click "Review."
  13. If necessary, go back to correct the parameters, then click "Finish" when ready

    Image 3
  14. Click "View in Resources" to see the tenant within the specified resource group
  15. In the Resources tab, the solution's status is "Processing" and will change to "Ready" once provisioning is complete
  16. The system owner of SAP Business Data Cloud, specified during provisioning, receives an email notification when the tenant is provisioned

    Image 4
  17. Once the provisioning is complete, you can see the newly provisioned tenant under the ‘Customer Landscape’ Tab and can also access the tenant directly through the tenant link provided there

    Image 5
  18. Now, once you return to the ‘Application tab’, the tiles of the products included in the SAP Business Data Cloud product suite become enabled, and you can start provisioning them 

    Image 6
  19. Once, the tenant is ready, you will be able to access the tenant from the link under the ‘Resource’ tab and under the tenant details
  20. Under ‘Customer Landscape’, you can see the system details of the new tenant. Also, the system integration is visually presented, where it is easier to understand and navigate through your system landscape

    Image 7
  21. As a next step, you will have to create ‘Formation’, integrate the SAP Business Datacloud cockpit/ tenant and other provisioned tenants 
  22. Navigate to me.sap.com > Products & portfolio > Business Data Cloud > Customer Landscape > Formation. Click on ‘Create Formation’

    Image 8
  23. Fill in the Formation Name and Formation Type

    Image 9
  24. Then select the BDC tenant and add it in this particular formation to work in sync
  25. Please click on Next and proceed with the formation creation

    Image 10

    Image 11

     

    De-provision Tenants

    Once SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud tenants have been provisioned in SAP for Me, you cannot deprovision them by yourself.

    To deprovision these tenants, proceed as follows:

    1. If any SAP Business Data Cloud content already exists in the SAP Datasphere or SAP Analytics Cloud tenant, uninstall all insight apps / deactivate all data packages, and then remove all related objects from the tenant.

    If catalogs have been mounted to the SAP Databricks Unity Catalog, unmount them.

    1. Submit a case to SAP Support and request that the tenant be deprovisioned. Please specify this support code in the case:  BDC-CPT-PROV.

    Provisioning Support

    In case of issues with provisioning, open a support ticket

    Please specify the appropriate support code in the ticket, depending on the issue at hand:

    SAP Business Data Cloud Provisioning - General & Cockpit : BDC-CPT-PROV

    SAP Datasphere: DS-PROV

    SAP Analytics Cloud: LOD-ANA-ADM-APS

    SAP Databricks: See Requesting Help from SAP Technical Support.

entry.title: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud : SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
As a continuation of the series of the Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud and its various components, this blog will help you in provisioning SAP Datasphere within Business Data Cloud. Please refer to the different parts to get a holistic understanding of the entire process.

Part 1: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud through SAP4Me
Part 2: Provisioning of SAP Datasphere
Part 3: Provisioning of SAP Analytics Cloud
Part 4: Provisioning of SAP Databricks
Part 5: Installation of Intelligent Apps and Data Packages

#Part 2

In this blog, we will discuss in detail how to provision SAP Datasphere via Business Data Cloud Core.

  1. Begin the provisioning of BDC apps individually, for example, you can start with SAP Datasphere.

    Image 1
  2. In the "Provision SAP Datasphere” dialog that appears, provide details such as Solution Name, Resource Group, and Business Type

    Image 2
  3. Create a Resource Group, if it is not already created
  4. Select the same region and data center that you have previously chosen for provisioning the BDC Cockpit
  5. In the Quota Assignment section, specify the quota you would like to assign from the total capacity units available for the SAP Business Data Cloud solution. This, of course, depends on your business use

    Image 3
    NOTE: You must provide a minimum of 1532 Capacity Units. For more information about Capacity Unit calculation, see SAP Datasphere Capacity Unit Estimator

  6. Input details in the configure parameters section, such as the System owner's email ID and name. As a next step, review them, and then submit the information

    Image 4
  7. A confirmation message informs you that the provisioning request has been sent. Click "View in Resources" to see the tenant within the indicated resource group
  8. In the Resources tab, the status of the solution is "Processing" and will change to "Ready" once the provisioning is complete
  9. The system owner of SAP Datasphere, specified during provisioning, receives an email notification when the tenant is provisioned
  10. Once the tenant is ready, you will be able to access the tenant from the link under the ‘Resource’ tab and under the tenant details
  11. Under ‘Customer Landscape’, you can see the system details of the new tenant. Also, the system integration is visually presented, where it is easier to understand and navigate through your system landscape
  12. As a next step, you will have to add the tenant to the ‘Formation’ that you have created already, and added your BDC tenant to it
  13. Navigate to me.sap.com > Products & portfolio > Business Data Cloud > Customer Landscape > Formation
  14. Choose the correct Formation and then click on ‘Include System’ and choose the Datasphere tenant to include in the formation, and save the changes

    Image 5


    Image 6

  15.  A single datasphere tenant can only be part of one formation and not multiple formations

    Now, you have successfully provisioned a SAP Datasphere tenant within the BDC Core.

entry.title: Re: Restriction of material sale in S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content: Hello, request is to restrict the sale of materials for few customers just for time being. There is an app "Create Material Listings and Exclusions", but it's applicable only for 1 customer what is not acceptable for the client because of manual work. Request is to restrict more materials for a list of customers.

entry.title: Re: Desktop Agent External Authentication: Failed to launch browser process.
entry.content: We have the same issue

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Why is SAP Desktop Agen...
entry.content:
Hi Rohit, thanks for your comment.

I can see that there is limited functionality available directly on a process, (including actions), but we need to access SAP Document Extraction which I've been doing via the SDK which is only available in an automation.

Is there a way around that?

entry.title: Manage Posting Periods - CDS View call
entry.content:
Hi,

An external system that provide SAP S4 Cloud with journal entries is in need of an overview of the Posting Periods.

In SAP S4 Cloud we can manage these via the app "Manage Posting Periods" where this app calls for the "FAC_GL_MAINT_POSTING_PERIOD_SRV/PostingPeriodSet" Odata Service.

How can we create an arrangement/CDS view for this Odata service?

Many thanks in advance.


Regards,

 

Robin

entry.title: Re: Upgrade from old SAP GUI to SAP GUI 8 Patch 12
entry.content: For information on how to silently install SAP GUI default package, please refer to the Frontend Installation guide: https://help.sap.com/doc/2e5792a2569b403da415080f35f8bbf6/800.08/en-US/sap_frontend_inst_guide.pdf

entry.title: ATC for AUTHORITY-CHECK
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,
     I have implemented in ATC if a program has AUTHORITY-CHECK, but when releasing the request it is only validating in Functions or Methods.
     When I release the request that has a type 1 program the verification is not performed.
     Can anyone tell me what is missing?
     Thank you very much
Regards

entry.title: Re: IAS Certificate: Configuring IAS SAML 2.0 Integration...
entry.content:
Hi 

This was supposed to be done automatically by SAP if you had IAS integrated but SAP recently released a note on this for a manual process if needed: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003595935

We're using metadata URL https://performancemanager4.successfactors.com/saml2/spmetadata?company=<companyID>

the new certificate expires in august 2029 in our case

entry.title: Re: Workflow approval via Integration Center
entry.content: thanks @StarNat

entry.title: Re: No certification even after a week of exam and support ticket
entry.content: Click "Contact Us" in https://support.learning.sap.com/#/

entry.title: Re: Stay certified exam error > SAP Activate
entry.content: thanks all , issue is solved from backend now & i am able to take stay certified exam. thanks!!

entry.title: Table FQM_FLOW not updated
entry.content:
Hi all,

I noticed a "special" behaviour for table FQM_FLOW having a PO with more than 1 items.

After saving the invoice for 1 item in the PO, the item does not switch to status deleted in FQM_FLOW table. According to my understanding the status should switch from FQM_FLOW-FLG_ACTUAL to FQM_FLOW-DELETED.

The status only changes when an invoice has been posted for all items. Is this the normal behaviour?

Regards

Guti

entry.title: Re: Automate User Assignment in Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi @KatjaHuschle,

thanks for this example. I wanted to try it out, but I am not sure how to deploy it correctly in SAP DM. Is there any instruction for deploying custom apps in SAP DM?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Condition contracts - vendor rebates
entry.content:
Hi @jonathanmarchant, 

To add on top of what Thorsten suggested. 
Yes, that's possible to post to a clearing account instead of the Supplier/Customer, for that you'll have to use Posting rule and maintain the account key required into the Settlement document type settings. 

The best practice would be to create a manual settlement document as suggested by Thorsten, to capture the invoice data received by the Supplier into a Settlement document. This will post to Supplier directly and reverse the corresponding accrual. 
Contract can then remain open if other invoices are expected or closed. 
If contract closed and open accrual remain open for this contract, then you can reverse the open accrual via WB2R_RAC or WB2R_RAV. Please note that the contract can't be reopen after the execution of this transaction. 

Regards, 
Gauthier Victor

entry.title: can we maintain same TM purchase organization to different company codes in TM org structure?
entry.content:
Hi TM Experts, 

We have customer scenario where they want to maintain same TM purchase organization for different company codes but i see we can only maintain one TM purchase organization under one company code in TM org structure. 

Kindly advise is there any way we can maintain same TM purchase organization to different company codes in TM org structure? 

Thanks,

Krish

entry.title: Generate dynamic file name from Email content
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Good day.
I would like to take your inputs about my requirement,
Existing functionality  : E-Mail( Mail adapter) ---> SAP PI ---> File Adapter( FTP)
SAP PI would read an emails from Email box and by using dynamic configuration it generates the filename and place it into folder

at present it generates the file name format is Example : Contract 2xxxx56_2xxxxx9953.pdf (this value comes from Dynamic subject parameter ex: (Contract 2xxxx56/2xxxxx9953)


but expectation of example file name is : Contract_2xxxxx9953 ( where we need to rid of this value '2xxxx56' in between)

Configuration settings on Mail adapter



Configuration settings on File adapter

 

what are all options for me to see with these configuration settings, that I can able to generate the custom filename with this example ( ex: Contract_2xxxxx9953)?

Please suggest me

Thank you very much.

 

With Best Regards,
Sateesh

entry.title: RAP View Entity: Analytical Table with subtotals/totals in List Report (SAP Fiori Elements)
entry.content:
Hey folks,

I'm facing an issue with totals/subtotals in a Fiori Elements Object Page table and would appreciate your insights.

I'm working with a Fiori Elements List Report application using OData V4. It's based on a RAP Custom Entity (unmanaged query) and includes an Object Page with a table section (Analytical Table) that is backed by a RAP View Entity. Both the List Report and the Object Page table are exposed in the same service definition and service binding (OData V4).

The issue:
I can't get totals or subtotals to show up in the Object Page's Analytical Table. Even though I annotated the relevant fields with @Aggregation.default: #SUM, nothing appears in the UI.

Here is a simplified version of the underlying View Entity:

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View Balances'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity Z_CONSUMPTION_VIEW
  as select from    I_BASE_ENTITY       as base
    left outer join I_COMMENT_ENTITY    as comment on  base.Field1 = comment.Field1
                                                  and base.Field2 = comment.Field2
                                                  and base.Field3 = comment.Field3

    left outer join I_HEADER_ENTITY     as header on  base.Field1 = header.Field1
                                                  and base.Field3 = header.Field3
{
  key base.KeyField1,
  key base.KeyField2,
      base.Currency,
      @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'Currency'
      base.Amount1,
      @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'Currency'
      base.Amount2,
      @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'Currency'
      base.Difference,
      comment.Text
}

Either all sort of errors occur (like: "FETCH API (direct DB access) is not supported for entity") or no totals are displayed at all. 
I've often times read that this only achievable with RAP OData V2 - where I could indeed make it work. Although I'm looking for a way in OData v4 with RAP. 

Probably you can help here, @Andre_Fischer 😉


Thanks in advance and cheers,
Max

entry.title: Something Went wrong
entry.content: One of my pages does this, any idea why this happens, but all other pages function well.

entry.title: Reason:UI5 is not installed or the sap-ui-version.json file is corrupted
entry.content:
Dear Sap we have issue seems SAP_UI is corrupted nting is working saml2/soamager all giving error 

Unable to retrieve UI5 version information from MIME repository

Message no. /UI5/CM_LOG003

entry.title: How to handle Inventory records with Sales order number on Sap DM?
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are facing an issue regarding floor stocks with Sales order number on SAP DM.

Our goal is to have clean stock, after the last production step is finished the product will be sold to the customer, the stock no longer exists on SAP DM (as on S4 side as well).

In general, we talk about process industries with process orders. Stock is created via goods receipt as this regular should be used. After the stock occurs on DM, this stock is also transferred to S4. In both system we can identify the same postings 1:1, the stock represents a similar picture.

Now we are facing the issue, when the produced quantity was delivered with a regular sales order to the customer, the stock on S4 is reduced (as our assumption was) but  the stock on SAP DM is still available and we start thinking about, how to clean up this stock.

For this case we tried to hand over before the delivery takes place this stock to S4. Via app” manage floor stocks” we assume to transfer stock from a production storage location to the central storage location using the Reason code “RTN-INV_CHANGE – floor stock return to ERP”, what is simply for records with Sales order number not possible.

We tried to achieve our goal (to eliminate the floor stock on DM side) also starting the procedure on S4 side, using a regular transfer posting via MIGO, movement type 311/E from the production storage location to the central storage location.
The result on DM side is an additional created inventory record, the stock is on DM side two times available (once on production storage location and once on central storage location created via movement 311/E).

May I do not understand the procedure fully, but how are these stocks consumed? From my understanding this finally created stocks never will be reduced in SAP DM, these inventory records stay there for a not known reason.

entry.title: Re: How to check if standard Fiori App is using SOAP API
entry.content:
No Fiori apps use or should use a SOAP API almost by definition.

For custom apps, of course that in your back end logic in ABAP you can trigger a HTTP request to a SOAP API - this is completely different thing.

For standard apps, there is no such a thing.

The API consumed by a Fiori app will be a REST API, 99% of the scenarios an OData API created either in SEGW, CDS Exposure, manual implementation of DPC/MPC classes or RAP. It can also consume a regular REST APIs which don't follow OData (for non-SAP APIs or something created in SICF) but a SOAP service is a huge mistake and it wouldn't be simple to consume in a web app.

entry.title: Re: Condition contracts - vendor rebates
entry.content: HI Gauthier, thank you for your reply. Where in the settlement document type do we maintain the posting rule and account key settings? We have been unable to prevent a KBS posting on settlement to the vendor reconciliation account

entry.title: Script that checks if values of a measure in a table are negative
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need help with a script that checks if values of a measure in a table are negative.

Let's assume my table is called Table_1 and I have a Measure, lets call it "Measure1" shown in a column in this table and a dimension "Dimension1" which members are shown in the rows of the Table.

How could a script look like that goes through each row of the Table for Measure1 and checks if a value is negative?

Ideally after the check there should be an if function, that triggers some Application Warning Message like  [Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Warning, "negative values"]  if the Measure Value is negative.

 

Thank you in advance!
Kind Regards,
Dennis

entry.title: Grouping fields names under database tables in Crystal Reports
entry.content: I know that in later versions Crystal Reports started grouping hyphenated field names by the first part of their name (e.g. Order-Num and Order-Date listed under Order) in Field Explorer under Database Fields (see attached screen shot), but I have a customer using Crystal Reports 2020 and it is not doing that. Is there an option or registry setting to turn grouping on and off?

entry.title: Re: How to check if standard Fiori App is using SOAP API
entry.content: There is a standard Fiori app that uses SOAP as per quick google search - F5418 - Schedule Sending of MRP Change Requests. The Fiori library clearly states that it uses SOAP API. Though this is the only fiori app I would find, might be wrong though so I was wonder if there are other standard fiori apps like with F5418.

entry.title: Re: Do you want to become or are new in the role of an SAP Enterprise Architect?
entry.content: Thanks

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors People Analytics (Stories): How to create change report (current vs previous valu
entry.content: This is very helpful thank you!

entry.title: Rust and ABAP ODBC
entry.content:
In past blog posts, we demonstrated how to use the ABAP ODBC driver with client programs like Excel and LibreOffice, and programming languages like Python (see links below).
In this blog post, I'll show how to employ the ABAP ODBC driver within a straightforward Rust program.
Rust compatibility begins with version 01.00.0018, but we recommend using the latest version of the driver for optimal performance and features.

To get started, set up a basic Rust environment following the instructions in the first chapter of the official Rust documentation.
This blog post uses version 1.86.0 (05f9846f8 2025-03-31) of rustc.

Begin by creating a new Rust program.

cargo new abap_odbc

Next, add the crates anyhow, text_io and rpassword to your program.

cd abap_odbc
cargo add anyhow text_io rpassword

The most crucial crate is odbc-api.
The ABAP ODBC driver does not support newer ODBC features, so you need to limit the odbc-api crate to older versions by using the feature odbc_version_3_5.

cargo add odbc-api --features odbc_version_3_5

The Cargo.toml file should now resemble the following:

[package]
name = "abap_odbc"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"

[dependencies]
anyhow = "1.0.98"
odbc-api = { version = "13.0.1", features = ["odbc_version_3_5"] }
rpassword = "7.4.0"
text_io = "0.1.13"

Proceed to insert the code for a basic program into the main.rs file.
Ensure you have a data source that is already configured in the ODBC driver manager, along with a username and password.
For further setup guidance, refer to the blog post discussing driver setup on Linux or the blog post discussing driver setup on Windows.

Start with a main function, where you can add function calls for specific examples later:

use anyhow::Error;
use odbc_api::{ConnectionOptions, Cursor, Environment, IntoParameter, ResultSetMetadata, buffers::TextRowSet};
use rpassword::read_password;
use text_io::read;

fn main() -> Result<(), Error> {
    println!("DSN:");
    let dsn: String = read!();
    println!("user:");
    let user: String = read!();
    println!("password:");
    let password = read_password().unwrap();

    let environment = Environment::new()?;
    let connection = environment.connect(&dsn, &user, &password, ConnectionOptions::default())?;

    // add the function calls to the specific examples here

    Ok(())
}

Run the program using:

cargo run

Depending on your setup, you may need to take additional steps to ensure that all shared libraries are found, like setting the LD_LIBARARY_PATH variable on Linux.

Initially, the compiler will issue warnings about unused imports.
At this stage, the program will prompt for the data source name (DSN), username, and password, and establish a connection.
The program should end without any additional output.

Next, incorporate a function that performs a simple SELECT statement and invoke it (simple_select(&connection)?;) from the main() function.

fn simple_select(connection: &odbc_api::Connection) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let sql = "select * from sys.dummy";
    match connection.execute(sql, (), None)? {
        Some(mut cursor) => {
            if let Some(mut row) = cursor.next_row()? {
                let mut buf = Vec::new();
                row.get_text(1, &mut buf)?;
                let ret = String::from_utf8(buf).unwrap();
                println!("{:?}", ret);
            }
        }
        None => {
            eprintln!("empty result");
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}

This function reads the sys.dummy view and outputs its contents to the console.

Since sys.dummy is the ubiquitous default object, currently, there's no necessity for actual tables or views in the system.
To maintain simplicity, the program only prints the first column from the first row of the result.
Conveniently, that's all that exists in sys.dummy.

Upon executing with cargo run, the output should be:
"X"

While this example provides limited functionality, you can now add a more complex one with a procedure call.
Our blog post Expose and Use ABAP-Managed Database Procedures (AMDPs) in SQL Services serves as a prerequisite for the next example since it employs the same database procedures.
Follow the instructions in the post to set up the required objects in the ABAP system.

Since the procedure requires an input table, create a local temporary table and populate it with data:

fn create_temp_table(connection: &odbc_api::Connection) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let sql = "create local temporary table sys_temp.items_in (item char(100) not null) on commit preserve rows";
    connection.execute(sql, (), None)?;
    let inputs_for_tab = &["Apple", "Orange", "Pear"];
    for input_for_tab in inputs_for_tab {
        connection.execute(
            "insert into sys_temp.items_in values(?)",
            &input_for_tab.into_parameter(),
            None,
        )?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

Once all inputs are prepared, proceed to call the procedure:

fn procedure_call(connection: &odbc_api::Connection) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let sql = "call zfmhorders.testprocedure(min_creation_date => ?, items_tab_in => sys_temp.items_in, id_tab_out => RESULT, items_tab_out => RESULT)";
    let min_creation_date = "20000101";
    match connection.execute(sql, &min_creation_date.into_parameter(), None)? {
        Some(mut cursor) => {
            println!("table result:");
            loop {
                let headline : Vec<String> = cursor.column_names()?.collect::<Result<_,_>>()?;
                println!("{}", headline.join(", "));

                let mut buffers = TextRowSet::for_cursor(500, &mut cursor, None)?;
                let mut row_set_cursor = cursor.bind_buffer(&mut buffers)?;
                while let Some(batch) = row_set_cursor.fetch()? {
                    for row_index in 0..batch.num_rows() {
                        let field = batch.at(0, row_index).unwrap_or(&[]);
                        print!("{}", String::from_utf8_lossy(field));
                        for col_index in 1..batch.num_cols() {
                            let field = batch.at(col_index, row_index).unwrap_or(&[]);
                            print!(", {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(field));
                        }
                        print!("\n");
                    }
                }
                cursor = (row_set_cursor.unbind()?).0;

                if let Some(new_cursor) = cursor.more_results()? {
                    println!("\nnext table result:");
                    cursor = new_cursor;
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        None => { eprintln!("empty result"); }
    }

    Ok(())
}

When the procedure call returns a result cursor (Some(mut cursor)), the function prints a table header with column names (header) and retrieves data from the cursor in 500 byte segments (cursor.bind_buffer(...), row_set_cursor.fetch()).
The data is then displayed in rows (batch.num_rows()) and columns (batch.num_cols()), and the loop continues to the next result table (cursor.more_results()).

Add both function calls to the main() function.

    create_temp_table(&connection)?;
    procedure_call(&connection)?;

The output should be:
table result:
ID, CREATION_DATE
0000000001, 2021-08-01
0000000002, 2021-08-02
0000000003, 2021-08-03

next table result:
POS, ITEM, AMOUNT
1, Apple, 5
1, Orange, 10
2, Apple, 5

While much of the content in this post is applicable universally when using the odbc-api crate, remember to limit the crate to older ODBC versions by employing the odbc_version_3_5 feature.

Related Blog Posts

Consuming CDS View Entities Using ODBC-Based Client Tools
Using the ODBC driver for ABAP on Linux
SQL Queries on CDS objects Exposed as SQL Service
Data Science with SAP S/4HANA – How to connect HANA-ML with Jupyter Notebooks (Python)
Expose and Use ABAP-Managed Database Procedures (AMDPs) in SQL Services

entry.title: Re: Automate User Assignment in Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi, thanks for your question. The sample implementation I have provided is a side by side SAP UI5 app. So you are not deploy it in SAP DM. You deploy it in any SAP BTP Subaccountand with the configuration of the Destination to the Public APIs you are able to integrate with SAP DM. Hope this helps.

Best regards, Katja

entry.title: Re: item not handled in batches and batch split item cannot be edited - TO creation
entry.content: Hi Same issue i am facing did this got resolved , please can you share the solution

entry.title: SAP шукає спеціаліста з розвитку продажів для української команди в Барселоні🚀
entry.content:
Приєднуйтесь до SAP як спеціаліст з розвитку продажів в Україні! 

Володієте українською та живете в сонячній Барселоні? Ми шукаємо талановитих фахівців для команди цифрового розвитку продажів, щоб разом створювати майбутнє взаємодії з клієнтами.

🔑 Основні завдання:

  • Проведення цільових кампаній через телефон, чат та електронну пошту
  • Прискорення розвитку воронки продажів та підвищення конверсії
  • Розвиток потенційних клієнтів та їх перетворення на кваліфіковані можливості
  • Співпраця з командами продажів та маркетингу
  • Планування та виконання дзвінків для генерації лідів
  • Передача кваліфікованих лідів відповідальним менеджерам з продажів

📌 Вимоги до досвіду та навичок:

  • 1-2 роки досвіду в генерації попиту або внутрішніх продажах
  • Досвід роботи в цифровому (віддаленому) середовищі з великим обсягом клієнтської взаємодії
  • Вільне володіння англійською та українською мовами
  • Готовність працювати в інтенсивному високопродуктивному середовищі

Зацікавило вас, або знаєте, кого може? Відгукуйтесь тут!

Станьте частиною нашого цифрового хабу в Барселоні та будьте тими, хто змінює правила гри у цифровій трансформації для безлічі компаній!

entry.title: Re: SAP C4C free trial tennant for study

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - Set App Variable
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have an app variable called 'Purchase Order Item' that is a list of objects. I have 2 pages ie. Item Details and Item Overview. Item Details page takes user inputs for an item and Item Overview page displays all the items as a list.

I have created a button 'Add Item' on the Item Details page so the item can be added to the Item Overview Page. In the logic canvas of the button, I have used 'Set App Variable' function to update my item list on Overview Page. This function is not appending to the existing items but rather replacing it. For example, if there is item 1 on Overview page and I try to append item 2 to it from Details page, it is replacing item 1 with item 2 rather than appending. Could you please suggest any ideas to resolve this?

Thank you. 

Kind Regards,

Praneetha

#SAPBuildApps

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello @Sushmitha_Yarramadha ,

Welcome to the SAP Community. Where are you based? Maybe there's a location group that you can join.

Check out SAP SuccessFactors Compensation tag and have fun learning and helping others.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Custom Code Impact Analysis Using ATC
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP states in SNOTE 2364916 - Recommended SAP Notes for using ATC to perform remote analysis: "For checked systems with a SAP_BASIS release up to 7.52, a central check system on SAP_BASIS 7.52 with the latest support package may be sufficient." What exactly does this mean?

Can I use a NetWeaver with SAP_BASIS 752 (latest SPS) without any limitations as an ATC central system to check a SAP system that runs on SAP_BASIS 740 (EHP7)? Or should I rather use a S/4HANA2023 (latest release) as an ATC central system if I have the possibility?

Thanks for clarification
Daniel

entry.title: Re: ABAP - Simple Transformation : Deserialization and op...
entry.content:
Let me ask again, any ideas?

In the official SAP DOC I found this:

The element tt:[d-]cond without explicit conditions can be used to flag
optional content in deserializations. This means, if a pattern does not exist
in the XML inbound stream, its evaluation is skipped without raising an
exception.

That's what I do...

entry.title: Re: Automate User Assignment in Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi, I think I expressed myself incorrectly, sorry. I deployed the app to the subaccount and not directly to the DM. I used the public-api-endpoint in the service key as the destination, maybe that's where my mistake lies. But the app should be displayed in the DM after deploying it to the subaccount, or how is it accessed?

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Issue displaying data in item list in Build Apps
entry.content: Hi Ludovic_MOOS, I'm also having the same issue with Set App Variable function. Have you managed to resolve it?

entry.title: Visualize os descontos realizados para o Crédito do Trabalhador em nosso novo relatório
entry.content:
Olá pessoal, 

Manter a transparência e o controle sobre os empréstimos relacionados ao Crédito do Trabalhador de seus funcionários agora é mais simples.  

Apresentamos a primeira versão do relatório "Visualizador de Descontos de Parcela em Folha", uma ferramenta desenvolvida para auxiliar no cumprimento de suas obrigações legais. Este relatório facilita a visualização dos dados de empréstimos do eConsignado, especialmente em situações de descontos incompletos devido à insuficiência de fundos.  

A solução é entregue através da SAP Note 3609743 - eConsignado - Payroll Installment Discount Viewer e utiliza as informações do infotipo Crédito do Trabalhador (IT3453) e da folha de pagamentos. 

Conheça o "Visualizador de Descontos de Parcela em Folha": 

O novo relatório oferece uma visão clara e detalhada de cada funcionário com empréstimo eConsignado configurado. Cumpra com a legislação informando seus funcionários sobre eventuais descontos incompletos. 

Funcionalidades da primeira versão: 

  • Filtros de seleção: Selecione facilmente o período e os dados dos funcionários que deseja analisar através de uma tela de seleção. 
  • Visualização em Log: Exiba os dados em um log estruturado e de fácil leitura. 
  • Status de Desconto: Identifique rapidamente o status de cada desconto com as seguintes categorias: 
    1. Dedução Total: Parcela integralmente deduzida. 
    2. Dedução Parcial: Parcela parcialmente deduzida. 
    3. Dedução não aplicada: Nenhuma dedução realizada. 

Funcionalidades previstas para próxima entrega: 

  • Exportação:  Exporte os dados em formatos como CSV. 

Compartilhe sua opinião! 

Se tiver alguma dúvida, sugestão ou comentário sobre este post, sinta-se à vontade para compartilhar na seção de comentários abaixo. 

Acesse também nosso workzone exclusivo dedicado para informações sobre o Crédito do Trabalhador. 

Não se esqueça de dar um "like" neste post se você achou útil e compartilhá-lo com seus colegas. Siga a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para se manter atualizado sobre as últimas notícias. 

Atenciosamente,

Anderson Marques

entry.title: Re: SAC Story hierarchy Node level data change
entry.content: Dear Raghu, could you please eloborate your issue with changing the values ? By default, it is possible to change values at any level in a table widget based on a planning data model.

entry.title: Re: 交易幣別與功能幣別的匯率只能預設抓取IMG的設定，是否有方法讓USER自行調整匯率?
entry.content: 可以手動輸入的匯率是「交易幣別」與「公司幣別」的匯率，並非「交易幣別」與「功能幣別」的匯率；請問是否有其他方法可以解決讓user自行調整「交易幣別」與「功能幣別」的匯率?

entry.title: Max retry and duration of retry by CIG / Spend Management Gateway
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are migrating from ECC to S4, during the go live period the SAP system will be down for 96 Hrs. / 4 Days. During this period the Business Network will be up so supplier will keep on sending the documents. During this period the transaction will fail in CIG. I would like to know how many times CIG re-attempts to send the transactions and what happens after max attempt is reached. I would also like to know how CIG handles sequencing of message so messages are reprocessed in correct sequence. 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Young Professionals Program in Portugal 2025
entry.content:
Are you a recent grad or junior professional (0-3 years experience) eager to jumpstart your career in tech and business? SAP, a global leader in enterprise applications and AI, is excited to announce its SAP Young Professionals Program in Portugal for 2025!

This free, two-month onsite program offers an unparalleled opportunity to:

  • Become a Certified SAP Associate Consultant: Gain globally recognized SAP certifications.
  • Receive Salary During Training: Focus on learning without financial stress.
  • Be Trained by SAP Experts: Learn directly from the best in the field.
  • Potential for Internal Hiring at SAP: Secure a full-time role with a global company
  • Join a Global Network: Expand your connections and opportunities.

    We're looking for graduates from 2022-2025 with at least a Bachelor's degree in IT/Tech, Business (Management, Economics, Finance, Logistics, Supply Chain), or Engineering fields. You must have the legal right to work in Portugal.

    This program significantly boosts the university's value by increasing graduate employability with certified, in-demand SAP skills and faster job placement. It builds strong ties with SAP, enhancing curriculum relevance and elevating the institution's prestige while contributing to Portugal's digital growth.

    Program Start Date: July 30, 2025. We'll begin calling candidates by early June 2025.

    Ready to start your journey with SAP?

    Apply here or send your CV directly: SAP Young Professionals Program Portugal 2025 Job Details | SAP 

    Help us find top talents and feel free to share this opportunity on your LinkedIn!

entry.title: Join Our Business Builders Roadshow | July 8 in Spanish!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to invite you to our Business Builders Roadshow, where we will be launching two new games on our platform! 

Join us for our regional roadshow, hosted by SAP University Alliances and HEC Montréal on July 8th 17:00 CEST | 11:00 EDT in Spanish, where we will provide you with a hands-on preview of the new games:

  1. The Maple Heir, a business fundamentals scenario developed by Prof. Michael Bliemel, and
  2. The Agent, a sports management scenario developed by Prof. Olivier Caya.

These games are perfect for students from school to university, making data visualization and data storytelling fun and easy to learn. 

You’ll have the opportunity to play these games firsthand and experience the fun and learning they provide. You’ll also experience demos, regional faculty testimonials, and Q&A, providing a comprehensive taste of the new games. 

Whether you are already familiar with Business Builders or are new to the community platform, this is a fantastic opportunity to discover how a gamified learning experience can help enhance classroom learning and develop SAP Analytics Cloud skills. By integrating Business Builders into your curriculum, you will be giving your students the chance to tackle real-world business challenges, empowering them to stand out in the job market. 

And the best: Business Builders is free for educational use. 

Register now and let’s shape the future of learning together! 

Check out other dates and times here.  

See you there!

entry.title: Re: Use custom Fiori Theme during BAS preview
entry.content:
Hey,

Thanks a lot for this brief blog, it was exactly what I needed, just a little mistake in `data-sap-ui-theme-roots` it should be

/myCustomTheme/UI5

 with the `/`. The screenshot is correct but not the text, it took a few try to spot this 😅

entry.title: Re: Automate User Assignment in Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi, you need to go to your subaccount where the app is deployed. In the space you find the app and there you find the URL. Click on it and the app will open in a separate Browser tab.  

Best regards, Katja

entry.title: Re: startup.bat is not recognized as an internal or external command operable program or batch file
entry.content: Any Answers

entry.title: Re: Key-user extensibility and updating fields
entry.content: code please.... what mapping you are talking? why do you think the mapping should be there?

entry.title: Understanding Stock Determination Groups for Consignment Stocks in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Consignment stock management is a crucial aspect of warehouse operations, ensuring businesses can track and utilize stock owned by vendors efficiently. In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), Stock Determination Groups help optimize the removal and allocation of consignment stock during warehouse operations. 

What is Stock Determination Groups

Stock Determination Groups in SAP EWM ensure efficient stock removal strategies by considering ownership and stock types during creation of Picking Warehouse task. During picking, EWM considers the warehouse no. stock characteristics Owner and Stock Type as defined by us. In doing so, it uses the stock removal sequence to check the existing stocks for the corresponding stock. The stock removal sequence arises from prioritizing individual stocks according to the valuation we define in Configuration. 

Let's check out an example of picking an consignment stock to get a better handle on this! below I would explain you with required configuration, master data and testing steps.

Configuration 

Follow these steps to set up a stock determination group specifically for consignment stock in SAP EWM:

  • Define a Stock Determination Group under the below path in SPRO: SCWM Extended Warehouse Management > Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Stock Determination > Maintain Stock Determination Groups. Create a new determination on group (e.g., CN-Consignment)).
  • Configure Stock Determination under the below path in SPRO. SCWM Extended Warehouse Management > Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Stock Determination > Configure Stock Determination.Few tips:
  • Stock owner has two types of values "Owner" and "Stock type". based on your requirement for picking you can maintain the value here.
  • In the Permitted value, you can give some value of the owner (consignment supplier business partner) or a wild card "*" can be used, blank value does not give any result.
  • Valuation in the above configuration determines the sequence of their usage, where the largest value is used first.

Master Data

  • Assign the Stock Determination Group in Product master. In transaction /SCWM/MAT1, go to warehouse data view under Stock removal update the Stock determination group.

Testing Results

Test case 1: Lets first test a case where we DO NOT maintain the stock determination group in product master.

  • Product master - do not update stock determination group.
  • Current Available stock, you can see owner is different than disposal party and Usage is C-Consignment.
  • Create an Outbound delivery with consignment product to be picked and Goods issued against any Production or Plant maintenance order.
  • Try to create a Picking WT but system wont allow you as the Owner of the available stock and owner in the delivery are not matching. System will throw an error

Test case 2: Now lets maintain stock determination group and do Picking for the same delivery.

  • Create Picking WT.
  • Picking WT is created and Confirmed. Goods Issue also Completed.
  • Later you can do the Goods Issue and complete the process.

Note: In above example, we picked and completed Goods Issue directly from the Vendors stock with usage as C-Consignment. Automatic transfer posting of vendor stock to our own stock and usage change from Consignment stock to unrestricted stock did not happened.  You are even not required to do the below configuration in the destination Storage Type and as such their will be no posting  with movement type 411(K) on S4 side.

Summary: Managing consignment stock in SAP EWM requires precise configuration to ensure seamless warehouse operations. By implementing Stock Determination Groups, businesses can optimize stock removal processes, enhance transparency, and improve inventory efficiency.

Would you like help setting up stock determination in your SAP system? 😊

entry.title: ONB 2.0: company-specific (individual) documents by using business rules
entry.content:
In our organization, we use the Onboarding 2.0 module to enhance the onboarding experience for our new employees. One key aspect that we aim to improve is providing customized (individual) documents tailored to each onboardee, such as personalized onboarding plans or"Your First Day" guides.

While we have successfully managed to provide company-specific documents, we are yet to find a suitable solution for individual customization. As we strive to create a more engaging and personalized onboarding process, we would love to hear how other organizations have approached this challenge.

Are there any best practices, tools, or strategies that you have implemented to provide personalized onboarding documents? Any insights or experiences you can share would be greatly appreciated as we work towards enhancing our onboarding program! Thank you in advance for your contributions.

entry.title: Re: Service call module update date and create time into...
entry.content: WOW! That worked. Thank you very much. I'll read up on FORMAT function again.

entry.title: Re: Settlement from WBS to cost center of the expenses only
entry.content: As stated in the error message you cannot capitalize revenue, it is a credit posting not a debit.  Use the source structure to settle the revenue to another object.  Revenue usually settles to profitability, but it's unclear why you have revenue posted to a capex project.

entry.title: Re: Template Allocation
entry.content:
ou’ve already completed the main steps, which are:

  1.  Created Overhead Key

  2.  Mapped Overhead Key to Overhead Group

  3. Maintained Overhead Group in Material Master (Costing 1 View)

  4.  Created Costing Sheet

  5. Created Template (KSC1)

  6. Assigned Template in OKTZ to Costing Sheet, Overhead Group, Plant

entry.title: Re: Onboarding - Learning integration for Pre-day1 New Hires
entry.content:
Hi @Parul3 

You could pick any field really and then just set a default value for it to be the same for all users.

I also believe you could just add a 'Fixed Value Field' in the IC job, instead of mapped from somewhere, and just enter the header as required as it is mapped (i.e., DEPARTMENT), and then set a default value for all. I have not tested this, but I believe it should work.
